```{r}
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00493740.1" = "chr6H49374",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00646960.1" = "chr7H64696",


# ---- Papain-like cysteine protease (uniprot)
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00313340.1" = "Epa",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00281420.1" = "Epb1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00281440.1" = "Epb2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00281450.1" = "Epb2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00281460.1" = "Epb2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00311260.1" = "CatB1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00495660.1" = "CatB2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00495670.1" = "chr6H49567",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00436460.1" = "Ale",

# =========================
# Leucine Aminopeptidase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00021950.1" = "chr1H2195",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00565260.1" = "chr6H56526",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00805360.1" = "chr7H80536",

# =========================
# Dipeptidyl peptidase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00100820.1" = "chr2H10082",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00348310.1" = "chr4H34831",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00406500.1" = "chr5H40650",

# =========================
# Tripeptidyl peptidase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00545020.1" = "chr6H54502",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00545030.1" = "chr6H54503",

# =========================
# Serine carboxypeptidase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00285590.1" = "Cbp1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00018290.1" = "Cbp2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00494930.1" = "Cbp3",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00222540.1" = "Cpb2-1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00222540.2" = "Cpb2-1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00597910.1" = "Cpb2-2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00107490.1" = "Cpb2-3",

"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00044340.1" = "chr1H4434",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00044350.1" = "chr1H4435",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00093780.1" = "chr2H9378",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00171840.1" = "chr2H17184",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00171910.1" = "chr2H17191",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00171920.1" = "chr2H17192",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00722350.1" = "chr2H72235",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00215140.1" = "chr3H21514",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00260410.1" = "chr3H26041",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00265950.1" = "chr3H26595",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00269040.1" = "chr3H26904",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00311090.1" = "chr4H31109",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00311110.1" = "chr4H31111",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00315620.1" = "chr4H31562",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00321770.1" = "chr4H32177",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00375970.1" = "chr4H37597",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00445740.1" = "chr5H44574",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00445760.1" = "chr5H44576",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00451630.1" = "chr5H45163",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00451680.1" = "chr5H45168",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00593540.1" = "chr7H59354",

# =========================
# Serpins (uniprot)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00314040.1" = "Paz1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00314080.1" = "PazX",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00476910.1" = "Bsz7",

# =========================
# Lipoxygenase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00307600.1" = "Lox1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00383870.1" = "Lox2-1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00579310.1" = "Lox2-1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.3HG00735580.1" = "Lox2-2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00760420.1" = "Lox2-2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00490920.1" = "Lox2-2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00519710.1" = "Lox2-3",

"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.1HG00050580.1" = "chr1H5058",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00156010.1" = "chr2H15601",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00188750.1" = "chr2H18875",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.2HG00188780.1" = "chr2H18878",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00307640.1" = "chr4H30764",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00369200.1" = "chr4H36920",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00369210.1" = "chr4H36921",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00462390.1" = "chr5H46239",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00462410.1" = "chr5H46241",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00462420.1" = "chr5H46242",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00462430.1" = "chr5H46243",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00462440.1" = "chr5H46244",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00481340.1" = "chr5H48134",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.7HG00616920.1" = "chr7H61692",

# =========================
# Allene oxide synthase (barkepgv2)
# =========================
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00359650.1" = "Aos1",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00359600.1" = "Aos2",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.5HG00466330.1" = "chr5H46633",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00493590.1" = "chr6H49359",
"HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.6HG00524700.1" = "chr6H52470"
)


# --- 1) Define the family-to-category mapping (in desired order) ---
# ================================================================
# Family → Category mapping (ALIGNED WITH all_families_ID)
# ================================================================

family_category <- c(

  # ======================
  # Amylolysis
  # ======================
  "Alpha-amylase"              = "Amylolysis",
  "Beta-amylase"               = "Amylolysis",
  "Limit dextrinase"           = "Amylolysis",
  "Isoamylase"                 = "Amylolysis",
  "Alpha-glucosidase"          = "Amylolysis",
  "Alpha glucan phosphorylase" = "Amylolysis",

  # ======================
  # Cytolysis (cell wall)
  # ======================
  "Cellulase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Endo-beta-1,3-glucanase"= "Cytolysis",
  "Beta-glucan exohydrolase" = "Cytolysis",
  "Lichenase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Xylanase"               = "Cytolysis",
  "Beta-xylosidase"        = "Cytolysis",
  "Arabinofuranosidase"    = "Cytolysis",
  "Mannanase"              = "Cytolysis",
  "Alpha-galactosidase"    = "Cytolysis",
  "Polygalacturonase"      = "Cytolysis",

  # ======================
  # Proteolysis
  # ======================
  "Subtilase"                         = "Proteolysis",
  "Papain-like cysteine protease" = "Proteolysis",
  "Leucine aminopeptidase"          = "Proteolysis",
  "Dipeptidyl-peptidase"            = "Proteolysis",
  "Tripeptidyl-peptidase"           = "Proteolysis",
  "Serine carboxypeptidase"         = "Proteolysis",
  "Serpin"                          = "Proteolysis",

  # ======================
  # Lipid metabolism
  # ======================
  "Lipoxygenase"          = "Lipolysis",
  "Allene oxide synthase" = "Lipolysis"
)
all_families_ID <- readRDS("Z:/PhD Munchen/Guido personal PhD Project_1/Barley germination proteome/Resources for Data analysis/R/R_Data/shared_malting_maps/all_families_ID.rds")

# remove families not present in the category map
all_families_ID <- all_families_ID[names(all_families_ID) %in% names(family_category)]

# --- 4) Collect proteins for each family ---
malting_rows <- lapply(names(all_families_ID), function(fam) {
  ids <- all_families_ID[[fam]]
  sub <- final_Hv5_mat %>% filter(Protein.IDs_pG %in% ids)
  if (nrow(sub) > 0) {
    sub$enzyme_family <- fam
    sub$Category <- family_category[[fam]]
    return(sub)
  }
  return(NULL)
})

malting_all <- bind_rows(malting_rows)

if (nrow(malting_all) == 0) stop("❌ No proteins matched the ID list!")

# ------------------------------------------------
# Map BARKE IDs → gene names (DISPLAY ONLY)
# ------------------------------------------------
malting_all$gene_label <- barke_gene_map[malting_all$Protein.IDs_pG]

# Fallback: if not mapped, keep BARKE ID
malting_all$gene_label[is.na(malting_all$gene_label)] <-
  malting_all$Protein.IDs_pG[is.na(malting_all$gene_label)]

gene_order_ref <- names(barke_gene_map)

malting_all$gene_order <- match(
  malting_all$Protein.IDs_pG,
  gene_order_ref
)

malting_all$gene_order[is.na(malting_all$gene_order)] <- Inf

# -----------------------------
# DEFINE ORDERING BEFORE MATRIX
# -----------------------------
family_names <- names(family_category)

category_names <- c(
  "Amylolysis",
  "Cytolysis",
  "Lipolysis",
  "Proteolysis"
)

# ----------------------------------------
# APPLY ORDERING BEFORE BUILDING MATRIX
# ----------------------------------------
malting_all$Category <- factor(
  malting_all$Category,
  levels = category_names
)

malting_all$enzyme_family <- factor(
  malting_all$enzyme_family,
  levels = family_names
)

malting_all <- arrange(
  malting_all,
  Category,
  enzyme_family,
  gene_order
)

# --- 5) Build numeric matrix ---
malting_mat <- as.matrix(malting_all[, ..int_cols])
mode(malting_mat) <- "numeric"
malting_mat[!is.finite(malting_mat)] <- NA

# --- 6) Row labels and annotation ---
rownames(malting_mat) <- make.unique(malting_all$gene_label)

annotation_row <- data.frame(
  Family   = malting_all$enzyme_family,
  Category = malting_all$Category,
  row.names = rownames(malting_mat)
)

# --- 9) Colors (unchanged) ---
heat_colors <- colorRampPalette(c("#FFFFFF","#FEE8C8","#D73000"))(150)

palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(family_names))
family_colors <- setNames(palette, family_names)

category_colors <- c(
  "Amylolysis"  = "#E6C229",
  "Cytolysis"   = "#7FB685",
  "Proteolysis" = "#E07A5F",
  "Lipolysis"   = "#8DA0CB"
)

ann_colors <- list(Family = family_colors, Category = category_colors)

# ------------------------------------------------
# Adjust column names to time points (repeated)
# ------------------------------------------------
time_labels <- c("0 h","12 h","24 h","36 h","48 h","60 h","72 h","84 h","96 h")
colnames(malting_mat) <- rep(time_labels, length.out = ncol(malting_mat))

# ------------------------------------------------
# ComplexHeatmap setup
# ------------------------------------------------
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(grid)
 
# ================================================================
# Heatmaps for correlation with enzymatic data 
# ================================================================

library(grid)
library(gridExtra)

panel_families <- c(
    "Alpha-amylase",
    "Beta-amylase",
    "Lichenase",
    "Limit dextrinase"
)

# -----------------------------
# Subset data
# -----------------------------
subset_multi <- malting_all %>%
    dplyr::filter(enzyme_family %in% panel_families)

if (nrow(subset_multi) == 0) {
    stop("❌ No proteins found for selected families.")
}

# -----------------------------
# Build raw matrix
# -----------------------------
subset_mat_raw <- as.matrix(subset_multi[, ..int_cols])
mode(subset_mat_raw) <- "numeric"
subset_mat_raw[!is.finite(subset_mat_raw)] <- NA
subset_multi$gene_label <- barke_gene_map[subset_multi$Protein.IDs_pG]
subset_multi$gene_label[is.na(subset_multi$gene_label)] <-
  subset_multi$Protein.IDs_pG[is.na(subset_multi$gene_label)]

rownames(subset_mat_raw) <- make.unique(subset_multi$gene_label)

# -----------------------------
# Z-score matrix (row-wise)
# -----------------------------
subset_mat_z <- t(scale(t(subset_mat_raw)))

# ================================================================
# FIXED CELL SIZE (global)
# ================================================================
cell_height <- 12
cell_width  <- 12

# ================================================================
# Helper: build stacked heatmap figure
# ================================================================
build_panel <- function(mat, title) {
    
    grobs <- list()
    
    for (i in seq_along(panel_families)) {
        
        fam <- panel_families[i]
        fam_rows <- which(subset_multi$enzyme_family == fam)
        if (length(fam_rows) == 0) next
        
        fam_mat <- mat[fam_rows, , drop = FALSE]
        
        ph <- pheatmap::pheatmap(
            fam_mat,
            scale = "none",
            cluster_rows = FALSE,
            cluster_cols = FALSE,
            treeheight_row = 0,
            treeheight_col = 0,
            color = heat_colors,
            show_rownames = TRUE,
            show_colnames = (i == 1),   # <-- ONLY TOP PANEL
            legend = TRUE,
            fontsize_row = 6,
            fontsize_col = 6,
            cellheight = cell_height,
            cellwidth  = cell_width,
            main = fam,
            silent = TRUE
        )
        
        # Remove any dendrogram remnants
        ph$gtable$grobs <- ph$gtable$grobs[
            !grepl("dendrogram", ph$gtable$layout$name)
        ]
        
        grobs[[fam]] <- ph$gtable
    }
    
    # Make panels closer together
    arrangeGrob(
        grobs = grobs,
        ncol = 1,
        heights = rep(1, length(grobs)),  # <-- compact vertical spacing
        top = textGrob(title, gp = gpar(fontsize = 14, fontface = "bold"))
    )
}

# ================================================================
# BUILD FIGURES
# ================================================================
panel_z   <- build_panel(subset_mat_z,   "Hv5 enzyme panels (z-scored log2 iBAQ)")
panel_raw <- build_panel(subset_mat_raw, "Hv5 enzyme panels (raw log2 iBAQ)")
out_dir <- "Z:/PhD Munchen/Guido personal PhD Project_1/Barley germination proteome/Resources for Data analysis/R/R_export/Hv5/malting_enzymes"

# ================================================================
# EXPORT (NO SVG)
# ================================================================
for (ext in c("pdf", "jpeg")) {
    
    # Z-SCORED
    out_z <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("Hv5_EnzymePanels_Zscore_fixedCell.", ext))
    if (ext == "pdf") {
        pdf(out_z, width = 10, height = 8)
    } else {
        jpeg(out_z, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 350)
    }
    grid.draw(panel_z)
    dev.off()
    
    # RAW
    out_r <- file.path(out_dir, paste0("Hv5_EnzymePanels_Raw_fixedCell.", ext))
    if (ext == "pdf") {
        pdf(out_r, width = 10, height = 8)
    } else {
        jpeg(out_r, width = 10, height = 8, units = "in", res = 350)
    }
    grid.draw(panel_raw)
    dev.off()
}
# ================================================================
# Bmy1 ONLY — 3 HEATMAPS (LOG2 10–30, RAW, Z-SCORE)
# Timepoints stacked (rows) × Conditions (columns)
# ================================================================

target_id <- "HORVU.BARKE.PROJ.4HG00380960.1"  # Bmy1

bmy1_df <- malting_all %>%
  dplyr::filter(Protein.IDs_pG == target_id)

if (nrow(bmy1_df) == 0) {
  stop("❌ Bmy1 not found.")
}

if (nrow(bmy1_df) > 1) {
  message("⚠️ Multiple entries found for Bmy1 — using first")
  bmy1_df <- bmy1_df[1, ]
}

# -----------------------------
# Extract numeric values
# -----------------------------
bmy1_vec <- as.numeric(bmy1_df[, ..int_cols])
names(bmy1_vec) <- colnames(bmy1_df[, ..int_cols])

# -----------------------------
# Parse condition + time
# -----------------------------
bmy1_long <- data.frame(
  value = bmy1_vec,
  colname = names(bmy1_vec)
) %>%
  mutate(
    condition = sub("_.*", "", colname),
    time      = sub(".*_", "", colname)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(condition), !is.na(time)) %>%
  group_by(condition, time) %>%
  summarise(value = mean(value), .groups = "drop")

# -----------------------------
# Build matrix (time × condition)
# -----------------------------
time_levels <- c("0hs","12hs","24hs","36hs","48hs","60hs","72hs","84hs","96hs")

bmy1_mat <- bmy1_long %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = condition, values_from = value)

# enforce time order
bmy1_mat <- bmy1_mat %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = time_levels)) %>%
  arrange(time)

rownames_vec <- bmy1_mat$time

bmy1_mat <- bmy1_mat %>%
  select(-time) %>%
  as.matrix()

rownames(bmy1_mat) <- rownames_vec
mode(bmy1_mat) <- "numeric"

bmy1_mat[!is.finite(bmy1_mat)] <- NA

# -----------------------------
# LOG2
# -----------------------------
bmy1_log2 <- log2(bmy1_mat)

# -----------------------------
# Z-SCORE (row-wise = per timepoint)
# -----------------------------
bmy1_z <- t(scale(t(bmy1_log2)))

# -----------------------------
# RAW (already linear iBAQ)
# -----------------------------
bmy1_raw <- bmy1_mat

# -----------------------------
# LOG2 CLIPPED (10–30)
# -----------------------------
bmy1_log2_clip <- bmy1_log2
bmy1_log2_clip[bmy1_log2_clip < 10] <- 10
bmy1_log2_clip[bmy1_log2_clip > 30] <- 30

log2_breaks <- seq(10, 30, length.out = 200)

# -----------------------------
# COLORS
# -----------------------------
heat_cols <- colorRampPalette(c("#FFFFFF","#FEE8C8","#D73000"))(200)

# -----------------------------
# OUTPUT DIR
# -----------------------------
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# ================================================================
# LOG2 HEATMAP (10–30)
# ================================================================
pdf(file.path(out_dir, "Bmy1_log2_10to30_timeRows.pdf"), width = 4, height = 6)

pheatmap::pheatmap(
  bmy1_log2_clip,
  color = heat_cols,
  breaks = log2_breaks,
  scale = "none",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  treeheight_row = 0,
  treeheight_col = 0,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  main = "Bmy1 (log2 iBAQ, 10–30)"
)

dev.off()

# ================================================================
# RAW HEATMAP
# ================================================================
pdf(file.path(out_dir, "Bmy1_raw_timeRows.pdf"), width = 4, height = 6)

pheatmap::pheatmap(
  bmy1_raw,
  color = heat_cols,
  scale = "none",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  treeheight_row = 0,
  treeheight_col = 0,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  main = "Bmy1 (raw iBAQ)"
)

dev.off()

# ================================================================
# Z-SCORE HEATMAP
# ================================================================
pdf(file.path(out_dir, "Bmy1_zscore_timeRows.pdf"), width = 4, height = 6)

pheatmap::pheatmap(
  bmy1_z,
  color = heat_cols,
  scale = "none",
  cluster_rows = FALSE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,
  treeheight_row = 0,
  treeheight_col = 0,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,
  main = "Bmy1 (z-score per timepoint)"
)

dev.off()

message("✅ Bmy1 heatmaps (log2 clipped, raw, z-score) generated.")
```


##Unbiased cluster analysis to see if there are group of proteins that are systematically more abundnat in more modern varieties  

```{r}
# ============================================================
# Identify proteins uniquely downregulated in Heat, Drought, or Combo vs Std
# (≥7 of 9 timepoints) and perform GO enrichment (Hv5 dataset)
# ============================================================

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.At.tair.db)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(VennDiagram)
library(grid)


# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load dataset and clean unwanted columns
# ------------------------------------------------------------
out_path <- "../../../R_Data/Hv5/pG_iBAQ_postnorm_bridgeOnly/Hv5_final.csv"
final_Hv5_mat <- fread(out_path)

# Identify intensity columns
int_cols <- grep("^iBAQ_", names(final_Hv5_mat), value = TRUE)

# Columns to drop completely
drop_cols <- c(
  "iBAQ_std_0hs_std", "iBAQ_std_0hs_r2_std", "iBAQ_std_0hs_h",
  "iBAQ_heat_0hs_r2_h", "iBAQ_std_0hs_d", "iBAQ_drought_0hs_r2_d",
  "iBAQ_std_0hs_c", "iBAQ_combo_0hs_r2_c"
)

# Remove them from the dataset directly
final_Hv5_mat <- final_Hv5_mat[, !colnames(final_Hv5_mat) %in% drop_cols, with = FALSE]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 2: Convert to long format
# ------------------------------------------------------------
ibaq_long <- final_Hv5_mat %>%
  dplyr::select(Protein.IDs_pG, TAIR, all_of(grep("^iBAQ_", names(final_Hv5_mat), value = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("iBAQ_"),
               names_to = "condition",
               values_to = "iBAQ") %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      grepl("_std_", condition)     ~ "Std",
      grepl("_heat_", condition)    ~ "Heat",
      grepl("_drought_", condition) ~ "Drought",
      grepl("_combo_", condition)   ~ "Combo",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    timepoint_h = str_extract(condition, "[0-9]+(?=hs)") %>% as.numeric()
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(timepoint_h))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 3: Average iBAQ per protein, group, timepoint
# ------------------------------------------------------------
group_avg <- ibaq_long %>%
  group_by(Protein.IDs_pG, TAIR, group, timepoint_h) %>%
  summarise(iBAQ = mean(iBAQ, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 4: Compare each stress vs control at each time point
# ------------------------------------------------------------
comparison_df <- group_avg %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = iBAQ) %>%
  mutate(
    log2FC_heat    = Heat    - Std,
    log2FC_drought = Drought - Std,
    log2FC_combo   = Combo   - Std
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 5: Identify proteins downregulated in ≥7 of 9 timepoints
# ------------------------------------------------------------
downregulated <- comparison_df %>%
  mutate(
    down_heat    = log2FC_heat    <= -1,
    down_drought = log2FC_drought <= -1,
    down_combo   = log2FC_combo   <= -1
  ) %>%
  group_by(Protein.IDs_pG, TAIR) %>%
  summarise(
    down_heat_count    = sum(down_heat, na.rm = TRUE),
    down_drought_count = sum(down_drought, na.rm = TRUE),
    down_combo_count   = sum(down_combo, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_down_heat    = down_heat_count    >= 7,
    is_down_drought = down_drought_count >= 7,
    is_down_combo   = down_combo_count   >= 7
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 6: Define unique and shared sets
# ------------------------------------------------------------
heat_only <- downregulated %>%
  filter(is_down_heat & !is_down_drought & !is_down_combo)

drought_only <- downregulated %>%
  filter(is_down_drought & !is_down_heat & !is_down_combo)

combo_only <- downregulated %>%
  filter(is_down_combo & !is_down_heat & !is_down_drought)

all_three <- downregulated %>%
  filter(is_down_heat & is_down_drought & is_down_combo)

cat("✔ Heat-only:", nrow(heat_only), "\n")
cat("✔ Drought-only:", nrow(drought_only), "\n")
cat("✔ Combo-only:", nrow(combo_only), "\n")
cat("✔ Shared in all three:", nrow(all_three), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 7: Venn diagram (scaled)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
venn_list <- list(
  Heat = downregulated$Protein.IDs_pG[downregulated$is_down_heat],
  Drought = downregulated$Protein.IDs_pG[downregulated$is_down_drought],
  Combo = downregulated$Protein.IDs_pG[downregulated$is_down_combo]
)

venn_out <- "../../../R_export/Hv5/unbiased/downregulated_venn_scaled.pdf"
pdf(venn_out, width = 8, height = 8)

assign("overrideTriple", 1, envir = .GlobalEnv)
venn.plot <- venn.diagram(
  x = venn_list,
  filename = NULL,
  fill = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"),
  alpha = 0.5,
  cex = 1.6,
  cat.cex = 1.4,
  cat.pos = c(-20, 20, 0),
  cat.dist = 0.06,
  main = "Unique and Shared Downregulated Proteins (scaled)",
  main.cex = 1.6,
  scaled = TRUE,
  euler.d = TRUE,
  lty = "blank",
  fontface = "bold",
  cat.fontface = "bold"
)
grid::grid.draw(venn.plot)
dev.off()
rm(overrideTriple, envir = .GlobalEnv)
cat("✔ Scaled (approximate) Venn diagram saved to:", venn_out, "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 8: GO enrichment (unique + shared)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
perform_enrichment <- function(df, universe, name) {
  genes <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(TAIR) & TAIR != "") %>%
    distinct(TAIR) %>%
    mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))
  
  if (nrow(genes) == 0) {
    cat("⚠ No valid TAIR IDs for", name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  enrichGO(
    gene = genes$TAIR,
    OrgDb = org.At.tair.db,
    keyType = "TAIR",
    ont = "BP",
    universe = universe$TAIR,pAdjustMethod="none", minGSSize = 1, maxGSSize = 50000,   qvalueCutoff = 1)
}

# Prepare universe
universe <- final_Hv5_mat %>%
  filter(!is.na(TAIR) & TAIR != "") %>%
  distinct(TAIR) %>%
  mutate(TAIR = sub(";.*$", "", TAIR))

enrich_heat_only    <- perform_enrichment(heat_only, universe, "Heat-only")
enrich_drought_only <- perform_enrichment(drought_only, universe, "Drought-only")
enrich_combo_only   <- perform_enrichment(combo_only, universe, "Combo-only")
enrich_all_three    <- perform_enrichment(all_three, universe, "Shared-all-three")


# ===================================================
# Step 10 — Simplify GO enrichment results for all sets
# ===================================================
library(GOSemSim)
library(igraph)

simplify_enrichment <- function(enrich_obj, name, ontology = "BP", cutoff = 0.95) {
  if (is.null(enrich_obj) || nrow(enrich_obj@result) == 0) {
    cat("⚠ No enrichment results for", name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }

  cat("\n🔹 Simplifying GO enrichment for", name, "...\n")

  # 1️⃣ Prepare GO data
  sem_data <- godata("org.At.tair.db", ont = ontology, computeIC = TRUE)

  # 2️⃣ Extract GO term IDs
  go_terms <- enrich_obj@result$ID

  # 3️⃣ Compute pairwise semantic similarity (Lin method)
  sim_matrix <- mgoSim(go_terms, go_terms, semData = sem_data, measure = "Lin", combine = NULL)
  sim_matrix[is.na(sim_matrix)] <- 0
  diag(sim_matrix) <- 1

  # 4️⃣ Build adjacency matrix for high similarity
  adj <- sim_matrix >= cutoff
  diag(adj) <- FALSE

  # 5️⃣ Create graph and find clusters
  g <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj, mode = "undirected")
  clusters <- components(g)$membership

  # 6️⃣ Select representative GO term (lowest adjusted p-value)
  selected <- sapply(unique(clusters), function(cl) {
    members <- which(clusters == cl)
    pvals <- enrich_obj@result$p.adjust[members]
    members[which.min(pvals)]
  })

  # 7️⃣ Filter enrichment object to simplified terms
  simplified <- enrich_obj@result[selected, ]
  enrich_obj@result <- simplified

  cat("✅", nrow(simplified), "non-redundant GO terms retained for", name, "\n")
  return(enrich_obj)
}

# Apply simplification to all four enrichment results
enrich_heat_only_simplified    <- simplify_enrichment(enrich_heat_only, "Heat-only")
enrich_drought_only_simplified <- simplify_enrichment(enrich_drought_only, "Drought-only")
enrich_combo_only_simplified   <- simplify_enrichment(enrich_combo_only, "Combo-only")
enrich_all_three_simplified    <- simplify_enrichment(enrich_all_three, "Shared-all-three")


##create an image from this analysis
# 1️⃣ Collect simplified enrichments
simplified_list <- list(
  "Heat-only"        = enrich_heat_only,
  "Drought-only"     = enrich_drought_only,
  "Combo-only"       = enrich_combo_only,
  "Shared-all-three" = enrich_all_three
)

# 2️⃣ Filter each enrichGO object to top 5 terms (keeping structure)
simplified_list_top5 <- lapply(simplified_list, function(x) {
  if (!is.null(x) && nrow(x@result) > 0) {
    x@result <- x@result %>% arrange(p.adjust) %>% head(5)
    return(x)
  } else {
    return(NULL)
  }
})

# 3️⃣ Merge filtered enrichments into a compareClusterResult object
merged_top5 <- merge_result(simplified_list_top5)

# 4️⃣ Generate dotplot (with -log10(p.adjust) color scale)
pdf("../../../R_export/Hv5/unbiased/GO_dotplot_top.pdf", width = 8, height = 8)
dotplot(
  merged_top5,
  title = "Top 5 Enriched GO Terms per Condition (Simplified)",
  font.size = 8,
  showCategory = 20
) + 
  scale_color_continuous(
    name = expression(-log[10](adjusted~p)),
    trans = "reverse",
    guide = guide_colorbar(order = 1)
  )
dev.off()


##Add annotations to the downregulated proteins 

ance_res <- enrich_ance@result
ance_res2 <- ance_res %>%
  separate_rows(geneID, sep = "/")

At_UP_annotated <- read_tsv("../../../R_import/Cam_At_project/Ortho_At_AtUP/UpAt_annotations.tsv")
UpAt_annotations_dist <- At_UP_annotated%>%
  distinct(TAIR, .keep_all = TRUE)
UpAt_annotations_dist <- UpAt_annotations_dist %>%
  mutate(TAIR = str_remove(TAIR, ";$"))

ance_res_anno <- merge(
  ance_res2,
  UpAt_annotations_dist, 
  by.x  = "geneID",  
  by.y  = "TAIR",
  all.x = TRUE
)

```

#Enzymatic tests on stressed samples 

```{r}

# ===============================
# GLOBAL FONT → ARIAL
# ===============================
theme_set(theme_get() + theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial")))

# ===============================
# A) GRAIN TRAITS DATA
# ===============================
grain_data <- tribble(
  ~Condition, ~TGW,   ~Protein, ~Starch,
  "Control",         56.72,   15.0,    57.2,
  "Heat",            55.79,   14.6,    58.4,
  "Drought",         45.24,   21.0,    42.0,
  "Combined",        40.81,   21.8,    41.4
)

grain_long <- grain_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(TGW, Protein, Starch),
               names_to = "Trait",
               values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(
    Trait = factor(Trait, levels = c("TGW", "Protein", "Starch")),
    Condition = factor(Condition, levels = c("Control","Heat","Drought","Combined"))
  )


# ===============================
# B) ENZYME DATA
# ===============================
enz_raw <- tribble(
  ~Condition, ~alpha_amylase, ~beta_amylase, ~limit_dextrinase, ~beta_glucanase,
  "Drought",   230, 78, 9, 179,
  "Drought",   214, 73, 10, 169,
  "Heat",      167, 45, 13, 133,
  "Heat",      167, 46, 12, 125,
  "Combined",  162, 80, 8, 152,
  "Combined",  172, 88, 10, 152,
  "Control",   187, 46, 22, 138,
  "Control",   187, 44, 18, 139,
  "Control",   188, 45, 22, 135
)

enz_long <- enz_raw %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(alpha_amylase, beta_amylase, limit_dextrinase, beta_glucanase),
               names_to = "Enzyme", values_to = "Activity") %>%
  mutate(
    Enzyme = recode(Enzyme,
                    "alpha_amylase"    = "Alpha amylase",
                    "beta_amylase"     = "Beta amylase",
                    "limit_dextrinase" = "Limit dextrinase",
                    "beta_glucanase"   = "Beta glucanase"),
    Condition = factor(Condition, levels = c("Control","Heat","Drought","Combined"))
  )

# ===============================
# C) PROTEIN-NORMALISED ENZYME DATA
# ===============================

enz_long_norm <- enz_long %>%
  left_join(
    grain_data %>% select(Condition, Protein),
    by = "Condition"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Activity_norm = Activity / (Protein / 100),
    Condition = factor(Condition, levels = c("Control","Heat","Drought","Combined"))
  )


#=====================================================================
# GLOBAL FONT → ARIAL
# =====================================================================
theme_set(theme_get() + theme(text = element_text(family = "Arial")))


# =====================================================================
# CUSTOM NATURE-STYLE THEME (UPDATED & CLEAN)
# =====================================================================
theme_nature <- function() {
    theme_classic(base_size = 20) +
        theme(
            text = element_text(family = "Arial"),
            
            # Facet labels
            strip.background = element_blank(),
            strip.text = element_text(size = 14, 
                                      margin = margin(b = 12)),
            
            # Layout & spacing
            panel.grid = element_blank(),
            panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines"),
            
            # Axes
            axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 10)),
            axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
            axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 10, angle = 45, hjust = 1),  # ← tilted labels
            axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 10),
            
            axis.line  = element_line(size = 0.4, color = "black"),
            axis.ticks = element_line(size = 0.4),
            
            legend.position = "none"
        )
}


# =====================================================================
# COLOUR PALETTE
# =====================================================================
palette4 <- c(
    "Control"  = "#FFFFFF",
    "Heat"     = "#E36B5D",
    "Drought"  = "#F2C75C",
    "Combined" = "#7EB5E6"
)


# =====================================================================
# PLOT 1 — Grain Traits
# =====================================================================
p1 <- grain_long %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Condition, y = Value, fill = Condition)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.65, color = "black") +
    geom_jitter(
        width  = 0.12,
        size   = 2.4,
        shape  = 21,
        fill   = "white",
        color  = "black",
        stroke = 0.5
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette4) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    facet_wrap(~Trait, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
    ylab("Value") +
    theme_nature()

ggsave(
    "../../../R_export/Hv5/carlsberg_Hv5/Hv5_GrainTraits_NatureStyle.png",
    p1,
    width  = 16,
    height = 8,
    dpi    = 300,
    scale  = 0.9
)



# =====================================================================
# PLOT 2 — Enzymatic activities
# =====================================================================
p2 <- enz_long %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Condition, y = Activity, fill = Condition)) +
    
    stat_summary(
        fun   = mean,
        geom  = "col",
        width = 0.65,
        color = "black",
        alpha = 0.85
    ) +
    
    stat_summary(
        fun.data = mean_se,
        geom     = "errorbar",
        width    = 0.25,
        size     = 0.5
    ) +
    
    geom_jitter(
        width  = 0.12,
        size   = 2.4,
        shape  = 21,
        fill   = "white",
        color  = "black",
        stroke = 0.5
    ) +
    
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette4) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
    coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
    facet_wrap(~Enzyme, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
    ylab("Enzymatic activity") +
    theme_nature()

ggsave(
    "../../../R_export/Hv5/carlsberg_Hv5/Hv5_Enzymes_NatureStyle.png",
    p2,
    width  = 18,
    height = 8,
    dpi    = 300,
    scale  = 0.9
)



# ===============================
# PLOT 3 — Protein-normalised enzymatic activities
# ===============================
p3 <- enz_long_norm %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Condition, y = Activity_norm, fill = Condition)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "col", width = 0.65,
               color = "black", alpha = 0.85) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.25, size = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 2.4, shape = 21,
              fill = "white", color = "black", stroke = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette4) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  facet_wrap(~Enzyme, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1) +
  ylab("Enzymatic activity (per g protein)") +
  theme_nature()

ggsave(
  "../../../R_export/Hv5/carlsberg_Hv5/Hv5_Enzymes_ProteinNorm_NatureStyle.pdf",
  plot   = p3,
  width  = 14,
  height = 4,
  device = cairo_pdf
)


```
